ew_context: Inverse head and shoulders completing corrective structure - likely end of wave 2 or B wave with resumption of primary uptrend
pattern_desc: Well-formed inverse head and shoulders with head at 108.85 and neckline at 114.16. Symmetrical shoulders and clean pattern structure support bullish reversal thesis
invalidation: Close below 109.73 on D1 timeframe would break the pattern support and negate the bullish reversal setup
market_context: AUD strength on commodity recovery expectations while JPY remains under pressure from BoJ dovish stance, supporting cross momentum
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

AUDJPY has formed a classic inverse head and shoulders pattern with the head at 108.85 and neckline resistance at 114.16. The current price at 113.33 is approaching the neckline break level, with symmetrical shoulders providing strong pattern validity. EMA20 at 113.21 above EMA50 at 111.91 confirms the momentum shift is already underway.

RSI at 44.2% shows neutral conditions with plenty of room for upside momentum. ATR of 1.05 reflects normal volatility for this yen cross, while laminar flow conditions suggest controlled momentum building rather than impulsive moves.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 114.16 (neckline trigger), 119.46 (measured target), 114.37 (swing high)
- **Support**: 111.30 (right shoulder), 108.85 (head), 109.73 (pattern support)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 109.73 on D1 timeframe would breach the stop loss and invalidate the inverse head and shoulders pattern structure.

## Macro & Flow Context

Commodity recovery expectations support AUD while BoJ maintains ultra-dovish policy keeping JPY under pressure. The laminar flow with momentum alignment supports the controlled reversal pattern development.
